column1: # Replace this file with your own user_gene_group_names.tsv at the same path
#   - First non-comment line is the header (required).
#   - Each row names ONE HGNC gene group, by NAME or by gg<N> HGNC ID.
#   group_input_identifier   What you type. Either an HGNC group NAME (e.g.
#                            "Collagens") or a gg-prefixed HGNC family ID
#                              name - treat group_input_identifier as an HGNC
#                                     gg<N> HGNC family id; look up by int(N).
#                              auto - let the script decide: if the value
#   group_display_name       Optional human-readable label used in logs and
#                            the side-car annotation map. If empty, the
#   notes                    Free text; ignored by the pipeline but preserved
# There is no HGNC group-name alias table (unlike gene-symbol aliases). The
# match against family.csv:name must be EXACT (case-insensitive, whitespace
# collapsed). If a row fails to resolve, the workflow writes
# 2_ai-unresolved_groups.tsv with up to 5 "did you mean..." candidates
# (substring + difflib search) and fails fast. Use the run's
# 2_ai-hgnc_group_catalog.tsv to grep for the canonical name and try again.
# By default, only locus types 'gene with protein product' and
# 'complex locus constituent' are aggregated (matches MODE 1 database
# workflow exactly). Pseudogenes, RNA genes, V/D/J segments, Ig pseudogenes,
# T cell receptors, endogenous retroviruses, and readthrough loci are
# DROPPED. Two YAML flags in START_HERE-user_config.yaml can expand this
#     include_pseudogenes: false        # set true to ADD 'pseudogene'
#     include_non_protein_coding: false # set true to ADD RNA/Ig/TR/...
# EXAMPLE (Collagens + Cannabinoid receptors + Gap junction proteins)

column4: all collagen genes (parent group; subfamilies aggregated via hierarchy_closure)
GJA / GJB / GJC / GJD / GJE subfamilies
